entry.title: Commit-Reveal for PQ Migration
entry.summary:
The proposed commit-reveal strategy for post-quantum (PQ) cryptographic migration offers a structured approach to secure blockchain transactions against future quantum threats without immediate disclosure of key details. This method involves a preliminary phase where participants can precommit to a PQ migration within a designated multi-year timeframe. During this stage, the specifics of the PQ keys or schemes remain undisclosed, ensuring privacy and security for the participants involved.
As the commitment window concludes, only those transactions with prior valid commitments are eligible for the subsequent migration process. In this reveal phase, participants disclose their PQ public keys and signature details, confirming their earlier commitments. This sequential procedure allows for early preparation without necessitating immediate proof of control over the assets, thus providing a strategic advantage especially for early adopters or those concerned with privacy.
Moreover, the flexibility of the design permits multiple redundant commitments per asset, enabling users to prepare for various potential PQ schemes and choose the most suitable option when necessary. However, this flexibility may entail certain risks in scenarios involving multiple parties, potentially enlarging the attack surface. To mitigate such risks, a foundational approach using Lamport-style signatures could be employed, with commitments specifically tied to these keys and the reveal process facilitated through PQ-enabled transactions.
This approach could significantly simplify the coordination typically required for PQ migrations while maintaining essential security features. It also ensures that only commitments made within the defined period are recognized, thereby setting a clear boundary for participation in the migration process. Such a system not only accommodates future technological evolutions but also respects the privacy and strategic preparations of the stakeholders involved.
